column1: PRM is a Personal Relationship Manager that enables you to manage your social network, keep track of daily notes, interactions, search universally using AI, and organize family relations. To see a list of all your contacts, navigate to the general page "/people"{People List} or "/"{Contacts Home}. Clicking on a contact will open their individual profile page at "/person"{Person Profile} (requires the contact's UUID).

Each contact can have linked social media profiles. You can view all linked accounts at "/social-accounts"{Social Accounts Page}. Selecting a specific account opens the profile detail at "/social-accounts"{Social Account Detail} (requires the account's UUID). Social networks are updated automatically or manually using scrapers.

Daily notes allow you to write journals and document events by day. To see a listing of all daily notes, navigate to "/daily-notes"{Daily Notes}. Clicking on a specific day opens "/daily-notes"{Daily Note Details} (requires the note's date/UUID). You can vectorize all daily notes to search them semantically.

You can upload, view, and analyze photographs. The photos are listed at "/images"{Images List}. Selecting a photo redirects you to the detailed page at "/image"{Image Detail} (requires the image ID). PRM-Face integration is used to detect and match faces in these photos.

The ELO Ranking feature allows you to compare and rank contacts. Navigate to "/elo-ranking"{ELO Ranking} to play a comparison game and see the leaderboards. Alternatively, you can play a gender guessing game at "/guess-the-sex"{Guess the Sex}.

You can configure Ollama models, Qdrant vector database connections, and tool permissions. To access these preferences, navigate to the settings layouts: "/settings/vector"{Vector Storage Settings}, "/settings/intelligence/tools"{AI Tools Settings}, or "/settings/app"{App Settings}.

The interactive family tree provides a visual graph of contacts and their relationships. Access the standard interactive map at "/family-tree"{Family Tree} or the advanced developer version at "/family-tree-dev-version"{Developer Family Tree}.
